title: move input_ids to hpu and remove disposal of adapter_meta
body:
# What does this PR do?

Fixes # (issue)

members/contributors who may be interested in your PR.

<!-- Your PR will be replied to more quickly if you can figure out the right person to tag with @


@OlivierDehaene OR @Narsil

 -->

step_plan.summary: Ensure Gaudi/HPU inference uses consistent device placement by moving `input_ids` (and related derived tensors) onto HPU at the right point in batch creation and adjusting token generation to avoid CPU/HPU mismatches. Remove unsafe disposal of LoRA `adapter_meta` in the Gaudi adapter loading/merging path so metadata remains valid across cached reuse and subsequent requests.

steps.operation: Move input_ids/attention_mask to HPU before building derived per-request buffers and ensure derived tensors have coherent device placement
steps.reason: The current creation/padding flow likely leaves `all_input_ids` (and per-request `r.all_input_ids`) on CPU while `input_ids`/`attention_mask` are later moved to HPU, causing device-mismatch ops on Gaudi and/or extra H2D copies. Moving to HPU earlier (or explicitly deciding which tensors stay CPU) ensures correctness and reduces transfers.
steps.side_effects: May change memory footprint on HPU (keeping longer `all_input_ids` on device). If `all_input_ids` is moved to HPU, downstream code that decodes tokens must copy slices back to CPU for tokenizer usage. Could affect HPU graph capture if tensor placements/shapes become more stable/less stable.

steps.operation: Align decode/append logic with new device placement for per-request token buffers (all_input_ids) and ensure tokenizer decode always runs on CPU data
steps.reason: `generate_token` appends next tokens into `req.all_input_ids` and uses it to produce text. If `all_input_ids` (or the appended ids) are now on HPU, any indexing/writes and subsequent decoding must avoid mixing CPU/HPU tensors; tokenization/decoding typically expects CPU lists/ndarrays.
steps.side_effects: Adds explicit `.cpu()` (or equivalent) copies for the minimal slices needed for decoding, which may slightly increase per-token overhead but prevents device mismatch. If `all_input_ids` remains on CPU by design, the step instead ensures all writes use CPU tensors and avoids inadvertent HPU tensors being written into CPU buffers.

steps.operation: Remove explicit disposal/freeing of adapter_meta and keep adapter metadata alive for as long as the merged/loaded adapter is in use
steps.reason: Prematurely disposing `adapter_meta` can invalidate cached references or tensors needed for subsequent requests (especially with adapter caching / reuse). Relying on object lifetime (ref-counting/scope) or storing required metadata in the returned model avoids use-after-free and intermittent adapter failures.
steps.side_effects: May increase peak memory usage if meta was previously aggressively freed; ensure that only necessary metadata is retained. Could require adjusting any code that assumed `adapter_meta` was disposed immediately after merge.

steps.operation: Stop disposing adapter_meta during adapter load/merge and ensure returned objects preserve any metadata needed downstream (cache-safe)
steps.reason: If `load_and_merge_adapters` currently disposes or triggers disposal of `adapter_meta`, it conflicts with caching and with downstream references from the merged model. Removing disposal here centralizes correct lifecycle management at the adapter utility boundary.
steps.side_effects: If callers relied on disposal for memory relief, memory may be retained longer. May require updating tests/monitoring for adapter hot-swap scenarios to ensure old adapters can still be GC’d when truly unused.

steps.operation: Remove any adapter_meta disposal in the cached merge path and ensure the cache does not store invalidated/disposed metadata
steps.reason: An LRU-cached `_load_and_merge` must never return objects whose internals were disposed after caching; otherwise subsequent cache hits can crash or produce incorrect outputs. Removing disposal (or guarding it) ensures cache integrity and stable adapter reuse.
steps.side_effects: Potentially increases cache memory residency. Might require revisiting cache eviction strategy or adding explicit cache clear hooks if adapters are frequently swapped and memory pressure becomes an issue.
